_meta.description: Fraction of total heat gains that can be usefully offset against heat losses. NRC methods return fixed factors (0-100%). PH Method calculates the PHPP utilization factor using gamma = gains/losses and the formula (1 - gamma^a) / (1 - gamma^(a+1)) with a=5.

inputs.name: radiantGains.utilizationMethod
inputs.description: Gains utilization method (d_80) - one of: 'NRC 0%', 'NRC 40%', 'NRC 50%', 'NRC 60%', 'NRC 80%', 'NRC 100%', 'Passive House', 'PH Method'

inputs.name: radiantGains.totalGains
inputs.description: Total gains (e_80) - only used for PH Method

inputs.name: transmissionLoss.thermalBridgePenalty.heatLoss
inputs.description: Thermal bridge penalty heat loss (i_97) - only used for PH Method

inputs.name: airTightness.heatLoss
inputs.description: Air tightness heat loss (i_103) - only used for PH Method

inputs.name: ventilation.netHeatLoss
inputs.description: Ventilation net heat loss (m_121) - only used for PH Method

inputs.name: transmissionLoss.components.subtotalHeatLoss
inputs.description: Transmission loss components subtotal (i_98) - only used for PH Method

preconditions.condition: method is one of the recognized strings
preconditions.description: Unrecognized methods fall through to default 0.4

behavior.output: (1 - gamma^5) / (1 - gamma^6) where gamma = totalGains / totalLosses
behavior.note: PHPP utilization factor formula with a=5. Clamped to [0, 1].
L'Hopital limit case when gains exactly equal losses

behavior.condition: method == 'PH Method' and denominator == 0 and gains > 0
behavior.note: All gains usable when there are no losses

behavior.condition: method == 'PH Method' and denominator == 0 and gains == 0

edgeCases.rationale: Mathematical limit of the formula as gamma approaches 1. Uses epsilon comparison (1e-9) for floating point safety.

edgeCases.condition: PH Method with zero total losses
edgeCases.rationale: Degenerate case - all gains are usable if they exist

formula.symbolic: g_80 = CASE(d_80): NRC fixed values OR PH: (1 - gamma^5) / (1 - gamma^6), gamma = e_80 / (i_97 + i_103 + m_121 + i_98)

references.source: PHPP (Passive House Planning Package) utilization factor formula
